reviews.review_text:
This place is awful. We were greeted very coldly at reception and interrogated about our dog’s suitability for caravan parks. Multiple signs around the park about theft made the place feel very unsafe. We have bikes with us and did not feel safe even with them locked to our van 3 different ways.
We felt extremely unwelcome, uncomfortable and unsafe in general.
The residents felt very icy and unfriendly, so did the park management. One staff member seemed to be doing constant laps of the park in his buggy, I assume to patrol for theft.
We got to our spot, had lunch, did laundry and left. So far this is the worst NRMA caravan park we have stayed in and now we plan on avoiding them all together.
Avoid this miserable park and go elsewhere.
Great carvan park

Great quiet location right on the beach with a short ferry ride over to Newcastle's bussling river front restuarants and bars. Cabins are spacious and tidy . Short walk to shops , cafes, and pubs. Will be back!
Excellent place for holiday
Awesome easy check on and booking.
Great place for a stay. Can get busy on weekends though
Beautiful location. We stayed 3 nights with a campervan, plenty of cooking facilities and chill out area. Good value for money with NRMA discount.

This park has a lot going for it but the chronic theft of anything not nailed down isn't one of them. Staff seem unperturbed by the regular thieving that goes on throughout the park, stating it's a common occurrence. We had our pushbike and clothing stolen. Another person had their car broken into and valuables stolen, and they are just the ones we know about. With no security, plenty of public access and no CCTV anywhere, thieves are having a field day. A security guard patrolling randomly at night might be a good start?  Obviously management don't care.

Ring ring... ring ring...
The pacific ocean called, they want their rotting seaweed back.
The stench of seaweed was so strong we had to leave in the middle of the night on the 2nd night. The st night was bad, but the 2nd was unbearable.
On pulling into the caravan park to find our site, the grumpy groundsman was very aggressive in signaling us to slow down. It was unnecessary as we were doing about 12km/hr.
Great off leash dog park nearby.
Beautiful place to camp and very friendly staff
Fantastic caravan park at a great location. Amenities always clean will definitely visit again. Best of all dog friendly

Excellent experience from start to finish. Asked to extend booking, very easy. Everything was exceptionally well maintained and clean. All staff were lovely. Easy access to beach and ferry. Bowls Club nearby for meals. Beautiful sunrises and sunsets
Great location with easy access to the beach, skatepark and ferry over to Newcastle.
Excellent Holiday Park. Clean, Well appointed and good management. Great location with a 10min walk to the Ferry to Newcastle CBD.

Cleaners don’t do there job and management is rude. Found a used condom (months old) underneath our bed in there cabins. Our 2 year old had been playing in our room with his toys when they went under the bed and my partner went to retrieve the toys when she noticed a used condom under the bed. With dust about an inch thick. When reported to the front desk the next morning on checkout we left it there after letting the cleaners know to go and clean it up before the next person arrived. Management then rang to be rude about rubbish bags left in rushing bags for the cleaners to take out on checkout no apology for how the room was given on check in. After spending nearly 3k there very disgusted.
Not enough showers. Great location and beach access. Great spot in Newcastle

The easiest check in for a camp site that I’ve come across. Shower was excellent with added rare bonus of a shower curtain so everything doesn’t get wet! Clean amenities. Generous sized sites but row upon row of them with no trees at all so not a great place to sit outside for views. Lovely to hear the sound of the sea so close by. Very well equipped kitchen.
Terrible
Feel free to walk around in budgie smugglers and blend in .

HORRIBLE HORRIBLE STAFF!!!
Don’t waste your time going here! The staff treat their guests disgustingly. They double book sites and yell at the GUESTS for the inconvenience. Facilities are dirty and disgusting - have spoken to many people with the same experience, not sure how they’re still running.

We just spent 4 weeks at Stockton in our caravan, what a great park and town, the staff at the park were friendly and helpful, amenities were regularly cleaned. Being able to walk everywhere was such a bonus, great Iga ,butchers , dress shop, which i spent too much time in. Great meals at the pubs and clubs and right on the beach with the pool across the road. Gary the groundsman is very friendly and informative,  be hard to beat this park,  hopefully get back there soon.
Rimligt prissatt och nära stan (via färja). Stranden är inte speciellt fin, men absolut ett härligt ställe vid soluppgång. Aldrig sett så mycket snäckor :)
Please avoid site 94 if you can, its got to be the worst in the park. Otherwise the park was in a good location
Très bon camping.  Simple et efficace.  Tout est a disposition

Great location. Good size sites.
Managers are very helpful.
Close to shops, restaurants and ferry to Newcastle. Nice walking paths.

Literally the perfect location for an extended family holiday. Staff are friendly and approachable, happy to help where they can.
We stay in our caravan but have also stayed in the cabins. Clean, tidy and comfortable.
See you again next year 🇦🇺

pros: good base to be able to explore the Newcastle & Port Stephens areas/ if your a keen fisherman the breakwall just outside the park looked like the spot to fish/spacious site / direct access to beach / quicker to jump on the ferry and head over to Newcastle than drive (depending on where your wanting to go obviously)

cons: amenities required better cleaning / had to lock up/put away EVERYTHING outside our van(including washing!) as their was nightly thieves throughout the park.

We stayed at the bequest of a family member, finally with a Van in tow, we booked the site months out and welcomed a week away, relaxing by Stockton beach.  Stockton was pleasant with a great coffee shop, brilliant restaurant at Club Stockton. We checked in using the parks pre-booking system which allowed us to drive straight in to our site. First night was eventful with a massive storm front arriving shortly after we finished setting up. The remainder of the stay was great, no issues and we have booked again from next year. If anything, I would suggest a refresh of the amenties block and more though cleaning of the walls in the toilets,  after a week, the same dirt was on the wall/s.

Good facilities and close to the beach. No swimming pool but there is a council pool across the road with entry fee of $2. I would have liked more activities for kids apart from the kids gym and swings.
A great caravan park, excellent location and lovely staff

By far the best camp site we’ve stayed in during our lap of Australia. The staff were super helpful and friendly. The sites were a good size. The toilets/showers were a little outdated but worked well and were always clean. We especially loved the huge camp kitchen which had everything in it that you would need. Even an air fryer! The adjoining TV room to the camp kitchen was an added bonus. Nice large room with board games, books, TV, comfortable sofas and tables and chairs. There was even an old school arcade machine which was free! Location is perfect.   Very close to the beautiful beach. The sunsets and sunrises were out of this world.

This is one of the best equipped and tidy caravan parks we have stayed at. The kitchen and the tv area were second to none and location to the beach an local amenities were fantastic i highly recommend this place.
Brilliant location, right on the beach and breakwater. Good amenities. Very popular. Only problem was the bindies. Everywhere. And the rest of Stockton was about the same.

This stay was 18/11 to 21/11/25.
Nice park but reception staff are nasty and not helpful at all.
We arrived at the allocated check in site with our van and walked into reception was greeted by a very rude lady who said you received all your booking details by email there is no need to come into reception. I advised we are driving and unable to receive emails and we always come inside to check in! She huff and puff and said everyone else can do it! Anyway after some time of pushing paperwork in front of us she explained very quickly where everything is and where our site was. I had to ask her to repeat and a bit slower but she was not happy about that and said it even faster!
I asked for the dump point and was told it is not working but should be fixed this afternoon! 4 days later it was fixed! We went out for a day and when we came back and pulled upside out van was approached by a man on a quad bike who said you are speeding and you have to slow down, we apologised and said we didn’t think we where going over 10 klms but he insisted we where. He then SPED off on his quad bike NOT doing the 10klms. We then sat outside of the van watching other drivers and most of them where speeding.
I feel we were victimised due to not completing the check in the way they feel it should be.
Also there was graffiti throughout the men’s toilets.

Awesome place to spend some time in Newcastle, beaches, parks, fishing, dining, the locals are friendly and accommodating of travellers, the Park is well kept and has some great amenities, the area its situated is 5☆

Charged us $80 for ensuite including the NRMA discount is expensive. No swimming pool however close to public pool and beach. Hardly any shade. No amusement for kids. Bin is not readily available. Dog friendly
Clean, close to Stockton pool,beaches and all restaurants, pubs and shops
Ensuite powered sites are great.
Great place to stay when visiting Newcastle or day trip to Nelsons Bay, etc. Moments to Stockton Beach ⛱️
wir waren sehr zufrieden:)

Netter Empfang, Danke. Wir durften ein Verlängerungskabel ausleihen.
Es hat uns sehr gut gefallen.
Needs a lot of maintenance great staff

Had a beautiful time!!

Walking distance to a few restaurants - lexies , bowling club, etc 😍

The beach is right in front of you.

With playground and games room it’s all you need for a nice time w the kids!!
Excellent service and facilities. Very professional staff and pre arrival check in info. Great location especially for us travelling with a pooch. We will be back for sure and for longer.
We were in a caravan, powered site. The park and staff lovely. Clean toilets and showers, Close to shops and Bowls club for nice meals.

In love with this campsite, the calm, the people who attended us super kindly at reception, the direct exit to the beach. The tent sites are huge and super comfortable, the amenities clean. I loved everything, Newcastle in general.

Awesome place to stay space was awesome. Very close to Stockton Beach four-wheel-drive entry plus got to use my generator at night which was amazing. Staff are very friendly would come again and would recommend it to anyone. Awesome park.

Visited Stockton Beach Park to look at sites available for short stay in the future. Lots of sites for large & small vans. Close to beach and walking distance to ferry and local pub.
Amenities are very clean and dog friendly, and staff member was very helpful and friendly.
A great location, friendly staff

Great location for the beach.
Staff are friendly and helpful.
Sites are large.

NRMA Stockton beach is a nice tucked away place. Quiet beautiful and only 5 minutes to New Castle by ferry.

The people are nice and very polite.  A good getaway from Sydney in a little over 2 hours. Depending on where in Sydney you live, of course.

Dolphins can be seen close to shore, and the beach is pleasant on a hot day.

It's not a bad spot to fish, mainly flat head. The constant in flux of commercial ships is fun to watch, and hearing their horns blow as they pull in is a novelty.

It's a bit of a drive from Stockton to Newcastle if u need supplies.

Overall, it's not a bad place for a short holiday.
Large sites right next to the beach. Easy walk to the local shops and the ferry to Newcastle.
Love this park, direct access to beach, big powered sites will go again
Beach besides us, not much shade. Watch out for taps when parking. Have concrete or grass site's.

Nrma caravan parks are always well organised and in prime location right on the beach. Mostly it was quiet but a lot of kids on bikes that don't follow any rules at all whilst management keep reminding grown ups to drive slow etc the kids are speeding

Google
Nice place for swimming and fishing. You can hear the waves at night and its quite calming. However, my unfortunate experience was the fact some of the bug netted windows had holes in them. Because of that, I was swarmed by bugs when I tried to sleep. Another fact is, me and my family entered the park and some crazy staff member accused us of speeding when we were well in the required limits. And when I was trying not to laugh at her outrageous accusation, she threatened to remove us and our car from the park. 1/5 experience. Only good part was the how close we could fish the rest was shit.
Awesome camp kitchen|Generous size sites|Very well maintained and clean|Great location for Stockton beach|Walking distance to shops and ferry terminal
I get out 1 hour late for exit, and i said apologies but customer service so rude. I never come back there. The Camp site was so nice but the customer service no.
Great kid and dog friendly. Park is very clean. close to bowling club, cafes and restaurants. Walk to beach also dog friendly
Great location, huge sites. Little bit $$ as most coastal parks are.

We stayed here due the poor reviews of other parks inside Newcastle. Right beside the ocean, so great location and close to catch the ferry across the harbour. The amenities are great, the kitchen is superb! However it is expensive! $100 a night for 2A2C is the most we’ve paid anywhere unless it’s like Cairns with a full water park etc. This park has nothing of the kind to command such a premium, other than supply and demand.

We travelled from Brisbane and had a fantastic stay at Stockton Beach Holiday Park. The staff were friendly and accommodating, the sites were clean and well-kept, and the amenities were spotless. The maintenance man was always cheerful and helpful, which really added to our stay.

The location is excellent, just a short walk to the beach and main street, with the ferry only about 15 minutes walk away. We enjoyed the amazing views, watching the big boats come in, the nearby café was great, and the playgrounds the kids enjoyed.

We didn’t get to try the bowls club this time, but it’s on our list for next visit. Overall, a great holiday park and well worth the trip from Brisbane.
Everything is old or not maintained very rude bald headed maintenance/keven better parks around

We've stayed here with the van a number of times. The sites are generally easy to access, and even in terrible weather, remained in pretty good condition. The facilities are quite new, and very clean. Excellent access to Newcastle city.
Enjoyed the whole area fishing ships locations great

The caravan park was very good. Our site was very wide, easily accommodating our van, with its awning extended and our tow vehicle. It would have been possible to put another vehicle on the site. The site was a little sandy. I guess it is difficult to keep it grassed on a sandy soil, winter and while being used. The amenities were clean and functioning. The staff were very helpful. I would recommend this caravan park.
Great van site close to amenities with easy access. Amenities were very clean and booking and check- in seamless.
Really good park right near the beach

We unfortunately arrived during very windy weather and it did not abate for a few days. Too cold to use camp kitchens and no awnings out. No shelter.  Also no internet in our part of the park. We did ask to move as we were there for a week but not possible apparently.

Location was fantastic! However the room itself, was not great. I got the 3 bedroom Lakeside Accessible Villa as I have a disabled mother and the bathroom floors are a slip hazard after a shower, fridge is not easily accessible and opens the wrong way for someone who needs accessibility and although they give you a wifi password, the wifi only works for 30 minutes before cutting off. When I asked reception about this they told me is “wasnt their problem” and I “need to bring my own wifi” as theirs isnt the best there. $800 for 3 nights in a cabin, you would think they could give you some internet, not rudeness. My first time here - and not the greatest experience.

The park was fine, amenities clean.
PLEASE replace the faulty speaker in the male amenities area! Excruciatingly annoying sound.
Amenities great.  Location great.

Stayed for 2 weeks and had my parents stay in a cabin and we had a powered site.

Amenities are clean, regularly cleaned but usually smell like bleach.
Camp kitchen is great, lots of space and hot plates, large oven etc.
Free arcade games. Has a couple of paid games, TV, ping pong etc.

Our site was flooded initially from the rain and then the drain was blocked for grey water. So we had to drain onto the grass which kept our site under water. Our caravan was sinking and had to jack it up out of the sludge. Management come to redirect our hoses underneath our van to ensure the site next to us wasnt getting wet even though it was aiming downward toward the road. I had mentioned repeatedly the drain was blocked and he told us to remove our privacy screen to dry it out. Even though we had constant bad weather.
He seemed very concerned about "caravan park etiqute" for neighbours (who were on the other side of the site with a motorhome) - their site was absolutely not wet. There was very little concern for the actual issue of the blocked drain which was the cause of our flooded site.
The stay overall was good, had a nice time, good location. Close to ferry and right on the beach. Can hear the waves and the ships coming into the port. Skate Park behind the park, there's a gate open during the day for side access.

Good for the price, we paid about $39pn with NRMA membership.

Cabin was clean, good location at the end of the park close to a Cafe and beach access.

Very well kept park! Loved the location… Quiet with beach views, then a hop-skip-and a boat ride into the city!
We’ll be back. Very helpful staff and codes on amenities block which I appreciated close to the beach.
This place was so rude and disrespectful they treat us differently just because we are Asian never going to this place again

The staff at this holiday park was so rude and racist we felt really uncomfortable just by asking the staff to enter and exit the gate because  we missed how to used the bom gate  code , the way the man at the reception always ask “ do you understand?” With the action of his . He was so racist , and not respectful. Overall we found our trip unpleasant because of the extremely discriminatory and rude attitude of the staff would definitely never recommend this place cause of the rudeness. We should be treated with respect and not being treated like we are nothing just because we are Asian doesn’t mean you treat us differently.
We got a powered site for our 22foot caravan right near the water 1 minute walk from the break water to watch huge ships come in the have access to the beach from the park its great!
Staff rude never going back there
Fantastic Place.
We tried to book in rang the phone number and the lady on the other end was so rude and arrogant won't ever go back
Slippery tiles in bathroom, otherwise ok.
Friendly staff, very clean and well maintained facilities.

We stayed in a villa, beds were comfortable, the kitchen good (a bit worn), the shower is accessible - the shower head a bit hard to get under especially if you are taller. I would recommend the park if you plan to stay a few days, but not for one night if you plan on travelling onwards, as its too far from the highway and the roads to get there from the highway are rough (plus there are roadworks)! We arrived after reception closed (road works delayed us), they will leave a key in the combination lock box - unfortunately the text about this didn't arrive, so we had some moments of panic, but there is a mobile to call. There is no food on-site (a daytime cafe on the edge of the park offers breakfast, coffee). Fortunately there were some takeaways still open on Mitchell st  and we had fish and chips. We were driving a motorhome, we had just picked up - so no bedding etc, make sure you call ahead to check parking, as its tight.
Great set up and with the beach just behind it's just beautiful.

The staff on duty on the night of June 22nd were absolutely unacceptable. The kitchen was supposed to be open until 9:30 PM, yet they started cleaning up around 8:00 and insisted that it closed at 8:30. I even heard a male staff member mockingly say to a female colleague, “See? I told you we should lock the door.” This kind of attitude is not only unprofessional but also shows complete disregard for guests.

English is an international language—don’t assume that speaking Chinese means we don’t understand what’s going on. During this trip, I stayed at nearly ten NRMA properties, and this was by far the worst experience. If management cares at all about their reputation, they need to take this issue seriously and address the staff’s behavior immediately.
Very nice facilities for families, there is a nice indoor cooking area, games and a beach just next to camp.

Lovely park. Great location. Very clean and large site.
It would be nice to have a privacy screen between site 93 and the dump point.
Lovely beaches and break wall walk.
We would definitely stay here again :)

Extremely happy with caravan park , friendly staff, great location,large caravan sites, easy access to ferry across to Newcastle, great bike trails,C/P has a cafe. Close to beach. Amenities are clean, large camp kitchen. Would definitely recommend staying.
Our worst experience with NRMA holiday park. Very rude behaviour by the staff.
Schöner Campingplatz am einen langen Strand. Nach New Castle kann man mit einer Fähre übersetzen.

A reasonable experience at this Caravan Park.
The Amenities were clean and tidy, The location and access in relation
to the Newcastle breakwater and Stockton Beach is very convenient.
The closest petrol station is approximately a 10 minute drive. The closest Coles Supermarket is approximately 16 mins away, so it's not super convenient but certainly not too far to travel.
I was camping with my 2 kids, so I can't comment on the rooms.
I thought the $58 charge to pitch 2 small tents in a powered tent site was excessive. Particularly considering it was the start of winter and the camp sites were running at approx 20% occupancy.
This charge included an extra fee for a 3rd person ( my 14 year old daughter ), which again I found borderline unreasonable considering the time of year, occupancy levels, and the fact we used no extra space than what was allocated for the camp-site.
The lights that remained on for the whole night ( I understand the safety/security aspect) kept the ambient light level inside our tents brighter than what was comfortable and conducive to a good night's rest.
Staff were friendly, and we had a good time
Great
Service was Great,  amenities were spotless,  huge sites. Such a Great Park 👍 👌 👍

A great park with great staff who were very helpful, friendly and accommodating. Nothing seemed to be an issue.
The amenities are modern and always clean. The hot water in the main block never seemed really hot would be my only criticism.
The location is perfect with a very short walk to the ferry to get across the river to the Newcastle CBD and all the cafes and pubs however Stockton itself has some fantastic eateries all in a very short walking distance
It is also a fantastic place to walk with pathways all around and a great dog leash free area including a small beach area.
Well worth a extended stay.
Thanks guys and girls.

We recently stayed on an ensuite site for our caravan, and it was fantastic. The location is what truly makes this place – right on the beach with easy access to the sand and surf. You can't beat waking up to the sound of the waves!
The ensuite site was convenient and clean, and the park facilities overall were tidy and well-maintained. It was a peaceful and relaxing spot.
Great camping ground on the beach. You can here the waves all night.
Very quiet and peaceful, despite being almost totally booked out. Good clean facilities.

This is a hidden gem.
We are from Newcastle so it's almost laughable to have a get away on the other side of the harbour but once you are there you feel a million miles away.
Amenities are great, sites are large, staff are friendly, beach is great for families.
Do yourself a huge favour and experience this place, you won't regret it.

If you like being treated with poor attitude and disgusting customer service upon arrival. Then see Garry at the front desk.

My Mum and Dad arrived yesterday, Dad had questions months prior to arriving, his 3 attempts to call were not retuned.
My Dad simply asked, is there a reason calls don't get returned?
Garry's attitude towards my Dad was appalling as he disputed ever having missed calls. Garrys poor attitude was enough that Dad asked for a refund.

Thankfully Mary, gave a refund on the spot. Mary was kind towards my Dad and apologetic for Garrys behaviour. Mary shouldn't be the one apologising.
Don't waste your time booking at Stockton Caravan park.
Pull your head in Garry and maybe it's time to find another job or retire.
I didn't stay but it's an awesome place to stop off and camp for a night or few 😅👌✨
Great location , Staff are friendly and helpful. They are welcoming and knowledgeable. Beach just there at your doorstep.

Great park in a great location. This busy park has one of the best camp kitchens we’ve seen. And it’s walking distance to the ferry to Newcastle which only takes 5 mins to cross the river. There’s a lovely beach and coffee and groceries all within walking distance. We enjoyed watching the boats and tugs going in and out too. It’s the perfect place to base yourself when exploring Newcastle and the Hunter Valley.
I have family coming to stay here I September.  I can't wait. This is a lovely caravan park.
Staff not very welcoming, no pool or anything to do, very crammed sites

We enjoyed a 3 night stay in a powered caravan site. The amenities were decent and the park was quiet during the night. We spent our mornings swimming at the beach (the afternoons were too windy). Visited the Stockton Bowling Club for lunch and enjoyed their food and the quiet atmosphere. We also took the ferry across to Newcastle for something different.
Well looked after park
Awesome friendly staff that go over and above.

Love this place! The caravan spots are soo spacious and really well maintained. As soon as someone leaves they give it a quick mow and tidy up. Right near the beach and brake wall too! Only downfall is its dog friendly but not for the most important times of the year.. Other then that, wonderful people and wonderful place!
Awesome spot, right on the beach. What a vibe

Enjoyed our stay here. Unfortunately it rained a lot, however the management continued to update guests by sending texts regarding road closures and flooding in the park etc which was good. They also had plenty of indoor activities for kids to do in the rec room with staff. Great access to beach! Would stay again!

17.1.2025
Wir sind zum 1.mal in Australien mit einem Wohnmobil und haben wirklich Pech mit dem Wetter. Es regnet seit 2 Tage und hier im Campingplatz gibt es wirklich gar nichts für diesen Regenzeit zu überbrücken.
Waschräume und Duschräume sind ok, aber für das Geld haben wir etwas besseres erwartet.
Good spot but terrible grounds brown grass Manager is rude not friendly thinks he owns the joint, needs new manger
Beautiful, relaxing spot with wonderful helpful staff

Do not suggest staying here. I’m was a return customer but will not be returning again.

Returned with friends and more of my family to be extremely embarrassed and disgusted with the service we received.
I personally Paid for 2 sites. Placed all our camping equipment on 1 powered site and parked our 2 vehicles on the other paid powered site to be left a note on our window to remove 1 car and parked  it outside due to 1 car policy although I paid for 2 sites which entitled me to the entry of 2 cars.  After calling the male manager said if I didn’t like it I could leave without giving a refund.

Did not send my son in law with his access code to the gate before arrival and wouldn’t let me book 2 sites under my name. Was left guessing what his code could have been.

Deleted our code on day 3 when rang the lady answered with a giggle in her voice and said she would send someone down on his arrival he accused us of not using our code to exit the park..

Showers disgusting, Toilets were dirty, toilet doors don't close properly and stinks of chemicals that are left in a bucket.
My daughter was told off by a worker for not mopping the shower she was using with my younger children while they were still using the shower area.

Left a day early as last straw was drawn when manager decided to whipper snip the site next to ours at 9am while we were having breakfast. When asked if he could do another site while we were eating so we didn’t have to eat grass clippings too he insisted he had to do it then.  we noticed he hadn’t trimmed other sites at all when we had passed them half an hour later

Manager speeds around the park at all hours of the night.
Constantly felt like they kept watching what we were doing.

Horrible experience will not be staying here again
For the money I paid could have stayed somewhere with better facilities and service.

Location is near beach but also a high wind area not great for camping equipment many people had damage to awnings and tent set ups.

Would suggest staying somewhere else as management really destroyed my holiday.
Beautiful spot great for familys

I was really disappointed with my stay at NRMA Stockton. The facilities were in a poor condition, especially the bathrooms and kitchen, which were filthy and clearly not cleaned regularly. The state of the amenities was so bad that we decided to leave early in the morning and head to another location just to find somewhere clean to shower.

I expected much better from this park. I hope they take steps to improve the cleanliness and upkeep for future guests. This is not on par with other NRMA parks
This is the perfect stop to move around Newcastle without being in town. It is right on the beach, so going for a walk in the morning also lovely

We only here for one night the site is very dirty and someone comes in at midnight so we have to leave immediately, it’s very dangerous, there are two girls beside us was scared too, someone
scratch their tent, so we have to leave together at 3am
Situated right next to the beach, the facilities here are magnificent. The showers are immaculate and everything works as it should. Good security.

We went to the unpowered sites, not impressed.  A bit dirty, unkept and is not the type of place I like, too many campervans and almost no green areas , feels like camping in a carpark. Amenities are nice an clean
Was lovely on the hot day beach could do with a little clean up. But other than that all good
Great service and friendly staff TK's

Still a great spot. Amenities are a bit tired (ladies) but were very clean. Mens were a bit grubby (showers especially)
Still keep coming back.
Very good lovely hosts

Over the last few years the permanent vans have been removed. However they are being replaced with long term residents in tents, caravans and cars. Many long term dogs (sites full of pet wee). Dead grass, large dirt patches, lots of plastic on sites post long term caravan left. Usual problems with long term, parking, encroachment and ongoing noise. This park has only one thing going now. It is the only Caravan park close to Newcastle. Since management changes it has gone downhill. Doesn't mean amenities aren't clean, but if you are a tourist the experience here has declined over four years.

Clean park and sites, great staff, excellent camp kitchen. Easy access to the beach. |Be wary with your children,  multiple times i used the camp kitchen and the hot plates were left on, could have just been the bogans staying there at that time.

Just spent 9 days in this park, very friendly managers and a well-run park including very good facilities, close to Newcastle lots of things to see and do. Best part was going for a ride and taking in the scenery.
Great place, a little outdated, (bathrooms need replacing) but overall fantastic!

amazing place to visit. rooms are clean and surprisingly spacious for the family to get around and enjoy your stay. shops are close by and the best view is just on your doorstep. The Beautiful views of the ocean and at nightime you will be able to sit out on your deck and take it all in and just enjoy.
definitely a place to stay.
Friendly staff and very helpful.

(한국어도 같이 썼어요)
My first campervan experience in Australia was with NRMA Stockton.

That morning, as we were trying to check out, the van wouldn’t start, likely due to a dead battery. That’s when Garry appeared like a hero.

He contacted the rental company and found a way to get the van running. Even when the jump starter cables were too short to connect, he held copper wires with his hands so I could start the engine.

I want to express my deep gratitude to Garry, who turned what could have been a moment of despair for my family into one of hope.

저의 첫 호주 캠핑카는 NRMA Stockton 이었어요.

아침에 일어나서 체크아웃을 하려고 시동을 걸었지만, 배터리 때문인지 시동이 전혀 걸리지 않았어요. 그때 개리가 히어로 처럼 등장했어요.

그는 렌터카 업체와 통화하면서, 시동을 걸 수 있는 방법을 찾았습니다. 점프 배터리를 가져왔지만, 선이 짧아서 연결이 안될 때에는 구리선을 직접 손으로 잡아 제가 시동을 걸 수 있게 해줬어요.

절망 속에 빠질뻔 했던 우리 가족을 희망으로 구해준 개리에게 정말 감사의 인사를 하고 싶습니다.

El lugar no está mal. Es bastante grande y con salida a la playa.

Pero: la limpieza de los baños no es la mejor. Hemos estado en otros NRMA Holiday Parks que nos han encantado y la diferencia es enorme.

Por otro lado, nosotros reservamos un sitio para la campervan sin electricidad. Había muchísimos espacios disponibles y nos dieron el último del camping, al lado de una puerta pequeña que sale a la calle y está abierta todo el día (se cierra en la noche) pero yo no me sentí segura. Porque cualquier persona podia entrar desde la calle.  Además nuestro espacio fue el único el sitio sin acceso a agua (todos los demas tenían).

Y en la cocina intentamos usar el horno y prácticamente no andaba, igual que muchas hornallas.
Nice park right on the beach, generally pretty busy but still relaxed
Great location by the beach.
No hot water to have a shower 😬
Perfect location and easy access to Newcastle suburbs. Reception staff could not have been more helpful.

Good park, right on the beach. Some power sites are very uneven.
Amenities are clean.
Great location, great beaches, enjoyed our visit

We stayed on a powered site. Great location and clean amenities. Easy access to the beach. Lovely walk to ferry that takes you across to Newcastle. Very friendly staff. We will be back to explore this beautiful area some more.
Beautiful location. Great friendly service. Our cabin was clean. Lovely little spot. Only downside was that the beds were horrible to sleep in.
Great park for the motorhome and dog 🐕 friendly, too.
Beach front beauty

Stayed at a powered van site. Lovely spot. The kitchen/lounge was amazing, especially in the wet weather with a bubs. Was great to chill in there, play some games,etc. Absolutely love that they have a family bathroom. Was a lovely change to be able to bath bubs aswell as us have a shower, all in the same room. Great walks along beach. Would definitely love to come back in summer. $4 a load for washing/ dryer. Loved our stay.
Great facilities. Nice and clean. Needs more hot water!

Not a bad park just old and had quite a few long term residents. This brought its own issues like unkept sites and unpleasant behaviour. Multiple verbal altercations between users over several different hours including middle of the night. The profanities were unacceptable and we considered leaving for our young children's safety. The staff were good when I bought it up but there was also nothing they could really do post the incidents. Totally brought the entire park experience down.
The incoming ships were really amazing to watch-after we realised what the massive horn was coming from.

The best thing about this caravan park was the location ! It was great that this caravan park had a cafe as well - lovely staff . The staff in the office were ok to deal with and we did get a good deal with four nights and pay for three . We had lots of fun with the kids taking the ferry into New Castle . Unfortunately we found that the bathrooms here were not kept clean . The condition of the bathrooms didn’t seem to ever get cleaned on our stay ( 4 days) besides having the bins emptied . I’m not normally one to give a bad review but the cleanliness of the bathrooms was appalling .

This was our first time with a caravan.  The booking process was so easy.  The site was very well maintained and so easy to back into.  The amenities where so clean and heaps of them.  The beach is just there.  There is a childrens playground that looked great.  We walked around and it was so nice.  We caught the ferry and went to Newcastle for the day.  Would highly recommend.

Friendly helpful staff. Clean amenities and great showers 🚿👍Free light shows at night when the tankers come in to port. Sea life off the breakwall are a constant source of entertainment. 🐬🦭 Site was a bit lumpy after the recent rain but staff helped out with reversing as I was solo.
Walk through on way to coffee but mobile cottages look great

Staying in our van so can't give cabin review but from outside they look good.
Great spot located on beach with easy access.
A short walk (15 mins) to wharf to catch ferry across Hunter River to Newcastle, with local shops just up the road
We'll be back
Great caravan park for a short or extended stay in the Newcastle area.

This is a lovely park.. the sites were well maintained & the ensuite was clean  & tidy. It was very close to restaurants which made it easy for us to eat out for a change. Would have loved to go for a beach walk but the rain stopped us ..I can see however it would be quite pleasant in good weather.. will have to come back to check it out !!!
We locked ourselves out after booking last minute. Staff were very helpful,even after hours! I rate it
Alwsys full so must be popular
Great Beach's scenic was wonderful, nice people there fishing was fun
I won't stay again. It's a nice enough park but having music blasted across the river from some random nightclub is not my idea of relaxing.
One of the best ways to get away get to Stockton Beach

Our first stop on our caravan trip from Sydney to Cairns to complete our Big Lap of Australia.||Great caravan park right on the beach.||Great amenities and very clean.||A short walk through the park to the beach and breakwater. Views of Newcastle over the river.||You can watch coal ships coming and going from here too.||It is a short drive around to Newcastle.||The staff were very helpful.||Tidy clean and reasonable rates.||

Great park awesome location and lovely staff
My partner and I stayed here over Easter long weekend with our children, bbq facilities and the kitchen were clean and staff were well mannered and helped us with any queries we had. Showers were great, nice hot water and amenities were kept clean. Kids had a ball riding bikes around and going on the playground with other children, though I think some parents that stayed forget they still need to look after their kids while on a holiday. All in all lovely staff great managers will definitely be coming back here again!
Had a lovely BBQ there with family that were staying  in the cabins.
Always a great stay.

Great place to stay .
Easy walk to beach cafes .

Visited friends over Easter weekend,great location,unfortunately the people running the park were unpleasant.Plenty of kids riding bikes enjoying themselves. I witnessed the staff yelling at some of them.I don't think they should be running a park if they can't handle this.Its where families go for downtime and to enjoy themselves.

Location is amazing, loved the park and location.
But unfortunately  the service and staff are very rude specially Gary he made our holiday horrible told the kids off scared the kids he made them cry in fear.
Also, staff drives the go-kart so fast they  nearly hit a few kids and pedestrians.
Gary  had physical alteration with a few of the campers.
Gary is a total nightmare for the park we've never experienced anything like it.

Enjoyed our 2 day stay. Sites large sites. Gate to beach and break wall. Easy walk to small township. Butcher shop sells awesome meat.
Bowls club and RSL have fabulous meals. BC have $15 lunches
Right on sea, peaceful and a working harbour,
A little tired in the laundry facilities in the first amenities block ,needing updating,  always a busy park , and an excellent location for access to Newcastle

$59 for an unpowered site that is the length of a car, SO over priced staff are so miserable and rude

definitely wouldn’t recommend staying here
Nice park, staff are very helpful. Clean amenities. Nice location. Dog friendly camp sites

Had a ensuite spot for motor home right next to a great camp kitchen   Used camp kitchen one night and walked to bowling club for tea next night. Clean well presented facilities
Most enjoyable stop over

Love this park great spot close to Newcastle. Stockton is very quaint. Staff very friendly and helpful, we needed an extension lead for our power cable which they happily supplied and delivered to our site. Very busy we wanted to stay an extra night, but they were fully booked. Pet friendly with a dog beach nearby. Gets a bit windy being next to the beach so be mindful of your awning.
We stayed here at this CP,  great spot,  would love to have stayed longer,   definitely going back!
Lovely location but expensive
Fantastic unrivalled beachfront location but no favours by council charging super expensive rates and not expanding size.  Guests  need book 2 years ahead for peak period.

Our 4th or 5th stay here, but first time in a cabin. We stayed in a Breakwall cabin. Very spacious and clean. Small kitchenette with every you need, stove, fridge, microwave, dishwasher but no oven. Great view of the beach. Handy location - close to cafe and the patrolled part of the beach.

We used the camp kitchen one night and it is amazing.
Great location but the park itself is very ordinary...

We thoroughly enjoyed our stay at Stockton Beach, all the staff around the park and at the reception were very helpful and friendly.
We will definitely be back for another stay

Have stayed here a couple of times now; right on the beach, good camp kitchen and clean bathrooms with plenty of toilets and showers. Water in the shower is nice and hot! The amenties blocks are perfectly situated. The park is nice and close to Newcastle CBD which is perfect for us. Lexies cafe is a bonus. ||Only downside is people going through rubbish bins and individual site bins within the park at 2:30am each night looking for empty drink cans for their collection. The back end of the park is easily accessible by members of the public which leads to randoms occasionally visiting.
Guter, sauberer Campingplatz, der aber preislich deutlich über dem vergleichbaren Durchschnitt ist. Liegt eben als einziger nahe an Newcastle.

Wanted to visit for a few days over new years as due to work we don’t get much time off and were told we can’t book as it isn’t a long enough stay? Never heard of this, gladly took our business elsewhere.
Great place! Staff were friendly and helpful.

Right on Stockton Beach but that's where it ends. Rooms were very ordinary. Shower that squirted out the side and lots of other broken things including wifi. And all for over $325 per night! Never again.

Beautiful spot, got 2 unpowered campsites between 8 people and was plenty of space. Excellent staff, ensured music was turned off at 10 but other than our own screaming jets until 10. It was quiet otherwise. Great fishing off breakwall and clean amenities. Great spot!

Thank you Gary, for looking after us so well! Awesome organisation for the Airshow weekend, from checkin to food trucks, the SMS updates, etc. And despite the rain we enjoyed the music on Monday afternoon. We love this park and keep coming back. The Bowlo next door is good for a feed too. 👌
Needs security to quiten the party animals after 10pm
Perfect park for your holiday"   everything is good.. kitchen clean toilet so clean.. very nice view ship going past and beach so clean..

Staff were friendly & helpful. Stayed on a powered site. Amenities were very clean .
Have stayed a few times . The beach is looking great 🤞
Great spot in the summer
Bit expensive. Not all people wanting a holiday are cashed up grey nomads
Short ferry ride to Newcastle. Good sites, busy on weekends, facilities clean.
A great caravan park in a great location. Beach side access, easy walk to Stockton with some great cafes and pubs. Friendly and helpful staff. Very well equipped camp kitchen.
Absolutely 💯 best friendliest people ever I'd love to visit again month after month
Caravan park,  no rooms,  nice staff, the sites need a bit of an update,  & the Italian food van is a rip off in my view,  the park amenities  clean & tidy.
My stay at NRMA was very comfortable. The rooms are good, everything is tidy and amenities are all well.
A bit outdated and very expensive for not that much. But a nice camp kitchen!

Tried to make a booking but my call was taken by Sharon, who has been mentioned on previous reviews. I did not proceed with the booking because it was such an unpleasant experience just to book.

I called back and provided feedback to Gary, the manager, who seemed to be going through the motions, possibly interested - or not.

It is a shame that service is not consistent here because the lady I spoke to yesterday, who sounded very different to Sharon today, was actually helpful. Such inconsistency does not make for customer service.

What a beautiful experience my family had at this holiday park. Nicole and Gary I’m reception were so friendly and helpful. Approachable and accomodating.
All amenities were very clean and tidy and the park is well maintained.
Highly recommend this destination and will be telling family and friends about this place. We had a great time and will definitely go back! 5/5 service! Our family got to see the seal that hangs out beside the rock wall and the dolphins also visited and gave some entertainment :)
Great central location,  really nice park  on a beautiful beach.  Also great to take  a ferry to Newcastle.

Our stay wasn't long enough!
We so prefer caravan parks over hotels/motels. So much more room to run around and explore, and that close to the beach - how can one argue with that?!
Our 'Villa' was so roomy and comfy. Very happy with the amenities.
For a 6 berth, the cooking amenities could be more BUT there is a BBQ 2 cabins away and the view and sounds of the crashing waves makes for a gorgeous night on the balcony.
I run very cold, so I had to use the 2 spare single doonas and the blanket in the cupboard on the bed, while husband runs hot so had the ceiling fan - best of both worlds for us.
I bring my own container of comforts from home, my own mug, sugar, tea, snacks for the 2 yo but there's enough tea and coffee for a couple nights.
The location of the park is very secluded- over the bridge and away from the hub of Newy but, if accommodated for right, it's perfect. We cooked our first night and went into Newy for our second night.
Most things in Newy are no more than 25 mins away.

Our stay wasn't long enough!|We so prefer caravan parks over hotels/motels. So much more room to run around and explore, and that close to the beach - how can one argue with that?!|Our 'Villa' was so roomy and comfy. Very happy with the amenities.|For a 6 berth, the cooking amenities could be more BUT there is a BBQ 2 cabins away and the view and sounds of the crashing waves makes for a gorgeous night on the balcony.|I run very cold, so I had to use the 2 spare single doonas and the blanket in the cupboard on the bed, while husband runs hot so had the ceiling fan - best of both worlds for us.|I bring my own container of comforts from home, my own mug, sugar, tea, snacks for the 2 yo but there's enough tea and coffee for a couple nights.|The location of the park is very secluded- over the bridge and away from the hub of Newy but, if accommodated for right, it's perfect. We cooked our first night and went into Newy for our second night.|Most things in Newy are no more than 25 mins away.
Got the most terrible site in the park and the amenities were disgusting my husband couldn't even use the showers
Splendid place to enjoy nature. Bring the kids!
Very nice park... great location right on the beach... friendly staff... plenty of room 👍
We stayed the weekend the park is so clean the toilets and showers are very clean it’s close to the beach and places to eat the park it quite and people are friendly I’m sure I will be back
Nice camp kitchen

Sharon the office lady told my wife she did not care that they moved our site then retracted the comment and said “oh I didn’t mean to say that. Aggressive from the start of the phone call, over speaking my wife whilst my wife remained calm and polite at all times. On arrival she verbally attacked me saying the my wife or what ever she is, is very rude. I then said actually no I was sitting beside her and she was lovely. No phone call to let us know they’d moved our site. No customer service at the park at all. Sharon, I’d give her 1/10 just for turning up to work. She needs to be educated how to treat people with kindness and respect if she wants to work in the role she tries to perform. The park itself was clean and tidy. Showers were pathetically cold. Not good for winter. Fantastic location. Beautiful area. Showers need to be cleaned early AM before the peak hour bathroom rush only to find out they’re closed for cleaning. * star for the office staff.

The Stockton Beach Holiday Park had great rooms with good facilities and comfy beds, some of the rooms have great views of  the nearby beach and a beautiful view of the bay and breakwater where nearby ships pass through. Each room has good, lockable doors and good safety, furthermore the rooms are kept very clean and sanitary.
Very well run park. Sites are very good. Fantastic camp kitchen.
Great park, Great friendly informative staff. Great clean tidy amenities, and a short walk to shops, pub and cafes

Had to check in late and was told I’d get a text with gate code. Never got that text. Was lucky enough to tail someone in before the boom gate closed. Was confronted by a staff member and I told them we had a booking, got that all sorted. Men’s amenities were dustier than a nun’s nasty. Was awoken by tree trimming and chipping at 7:30 in the morning right across from our camp, no notice from staff that this would be occurring. Decided to go out for the day but rushed back as a massive hail storm hit even though it was a cloudless morning. Returned to our tent 3 inches underwater as half the park had flooded and everything we owned was soaked. Didn’t even stay the second night as we had to dry all of our stuff. I understand nothing could be done about the weather but a heads up from the park would’ve been nice as we had to drive 45 minutes back from Newcastle

While units are available, we used this park for our caravan holiday.  A variety of site options with reasonable room to park. Lots of amenities and close to beach and options to eat out. Easy drive to most places of interest.
Staff friendly and helpful.
Excellent place with great managers and staff
Good service and friendly staff Tks

I’ve not even stayed here yet & im at my whits even with this place.
I booked a non power site 2 weeks ago, inquired if a spot was empty to which the staff informed me it was free. Perfect i said, I’ll take that for 2 nights.

I then call the other day to confirm all was good with the booking & confirm we still had the same spot to which they said “no, we moved you”. With no real explanation at all, the lady says she can move up next to the spot we had already booked at the beginning, then rudely hung up. I’ve since been moved 2 more times, when asking about why they moved me, they said “we don’t reserve  spots”. So going off their logic it should be first in best dressed, why would you go to more effort to move someone already in a spot instead of just booking another customer in a free spot. but oh no apparently they will just flip flop you around when ever they feel like it. Wouldn’t waste my time here again 👎
Great facilities, close to ferry to go to Newcastle
Good place to spend a few days. Clean park with  excellent staff. Right on beach. Newcastle foreshore very accessible with 5 minutes ferry ride. The RSL club is a must for a meal or two.
Very clean amenities. Right on the beach

A non planned RV stopover couldn't have gone better. Fantastic first up friendly check in experience and made to fee veryl welcome. Very clean & fresh showers & toilets with serious shower heads 😀. A 5 min walk to shops stocking all the bare essentials and 10min to the ferry terminal for the 4min ride to Newcastle. Would stop in again in a heartbeat. Great safe park👍👍👍
Very friendly. Clean and brilliant location.
